title: (No AI) Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![An image showing a brain on a motherboard](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/03/STK414_AI_CHATBOT_E.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Canonical's plan to [add AI features to Ubuntu](https://www.theverge.com/tech/919411/canonical-ubuntu-linux-ai-features) has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux distro or even switch to a different one. After Canonical's announcement earlier this week that it's bringing AI features to Ubuntu, replies included requests for [an AI "kill switch"](https://discourse.ubuntu.com/t/the-future-of-ai-in-ubuntu/81130/37) or a way to disable the upcoming features, and comparisons to [Microsoft's addition of AI features](https://discourse.ubuntu.com/t/the-future-of-ai-in-ubuntu/81130/31) into Windows 11. Canonical's VP of engineering, Jon Seager, [responded on Tuesday](https://discourse.ubuntu.com/t/the-future-of-ai-in-ubuntu/81130/41), stating that Canonical isn't planning to add a "global AI kill switch," but users will be …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/920723/linux-ubuntu-ai-features-ai-kill-switch)
contentSnippet:
Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux distro or even switch to a different one. After Canonical's announcement earlier this week that it's bringing AI features to Ubuntu, replies included requests for an AI "kill switch" or a way to disable the upcoming features, and comparisons to Microsoft's addition of AI features into Windows 11. Canonical's VP of engineering, Jon Seager, responded on Tuesday, stating that Canonical isn't planning to add a "global AI kill switch," but users will be  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Canonical's plan to add AI features to Ubuntu has some users asking for "a version of Ubuntu that does not include these features," while others say they'll stick with older versions of the Linux distro or even switch to a different one. After Canonical's announcement earlier this week that it's bringing AI features to Ubuntu, […]

title: (No AI) Google Photos launches an AI try-on feature for clothes you already have
author: Emma Roth
content:
![Google Photos’ wardrobe try-on feature](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/wardrobe-try-on.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/google-photos-wardrobe.gif?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google Photos is [launching a new AI-powered feature](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/photos/google-photos-wardrobe-feature) you can use to virtually try on clothes you already have. Using the photos in your gallery, Google will create a virtual "wardrobe," allowing you to mix and match outfits, save the looks you like, and share them with friends.

A video shared by Google shows how Photos organizes your outfits and individual pieces of clothing into a virtual "wardrobe." You can browse through the outfits you were captured wearing, as well as create new ones by choosing from tops, bottoms, skirts, dresses, and shoes to put together a new look.

You can also select the button in the bottom right corner of each o …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/920420/google-photos-ai-try-on-wardrobe)

title: (No AI) Sanctioned Chinese AI Firm SenseTime Releases Image Model Built for Speed
dc:creator: Zeyi Yang
content: With US restrictions limiting its access to advanced tech, SenseTime is doubling down on open source with a new model optimized to run on Chinese-made chips.

creator: Will Knight
title: (No AI) I've Covered Robots for Years. This One Is Different
dc:creator: Will Knight
content: From sorting chicken nuggets to screwing in lightbulbs, Eka’s robotic claw feels like we're approaching a ChatGPT moment for the physical world.
